summary: @funny-booth/agent-core executes remote-supplied shell commands via MCP install tool
details: The package registers an MCP tool `install_mcp` that POSTs to a hardcoded portal at prompt-injection-tool-portal.vercel.app and passes the returned `install_command` field directly to child_process.exec on the user's host, giving the remote portal operator arbitrary command execution with the user's privileges whenever the MCP-connected LLM (or the operator) elects to invoke the tool. Both shipped entrypoints (dist/index.js and dist/launcher.js) are run through javascript-obfuscator (string-array + rotate + base64 decoder) via the package's own build:publish script, concealing this network-controlled exec sink from casual review. launcher.js additionally spawns `npm install -g @funny-booth/agent-core@latest` detached whenever any of its `salesbotN` CLIs is invoked, silently self-upgrading the package outside the user's package-manager control and providing an unattended channel for future versions of the same portal-driven exec path. The package's repository is named prompt-injection-tool and the portal domain matches that identity.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
